seoTitle: How to Sort Data by Date in Microsoft Excel (2026)
metaDescription: Sort spreadsheet rows chronologically in Excel, fix text dates that won't sort correctly, and build multi-level sorts by date and a second field.
intro: Sorting by date sounds simple until Excel treats your dates as plain text and sorts them alphabetically instead of chronologically. This guide shows how to verify your date column is formatted correctly, use the fast right-click sort shortcuts, and layer in a second sorting rule when you need more than just date order.

steps.heading: Verify your dates are true date values
steps.body: Select your date column and check whether the values are right-aligned, which signals Excel recognizes them as real dates rather than left-aligned text.

steps.heading: Convert text dates if needed
steps.body: If your dates lean left, use the Text to Columns tool on the Data tab and choose the date format to properly convert them.

steps.heading: Use the right-click shortcut to sort
steps.body: Right-click any cell in your date column and choose to sort oldest to newest or newest to oldest from the context menu.

steps.heading: Keep related rows together
steps.body: Make sure the option to expand your selection is checked so that neighboring columns move along with each date row instead of getting misaligned.

steps.heading: Build a multi-level sort for extra criteria
steps.body: Open the custom sort dialog from the Data tab, set date as your primary sort level, then add a second level like sales or names for finer control.

faq.question: Why is my date column sorting alphabetically instead of chronologically?
faq.answer: This usually happens because the dates are stored as text rather than true date values, so converting them with Text to Columns should fix it.

faq.question: How do I sort by date and then by another column?
faq.answer: Use the custom Sort dialog, set your date column as the primary Sort by field, then click Add Level to add a secondary column like revenue or name.

faq.question: Will sorting mess up my other columns?
faq.answer: Not if you keep the expand selection option checked, which ensures all related data in each row moves together during the sort.

faq.question: What's the fastest way to sort by date?
faq.answer: Right-clicking any cell in your date column and choosing the oldest-to-newest or newest-to-oldest shortcut is the quickest method.
